markdown:
#  Void Budget Changes 
#####  Phased Release
The [new Budget Changes feature](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/about-budget-changes/) is designed to replace the existing budget modifications feature. Once you migrate to Budget Changes, you will no longer have access to the budget modifications feature. If you have any questions before your company starts the migration, contact your Procore point of contact. To learn more about the timeline for migration, see: [Common Questions](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/about-budget-changes/common-questions).
##  Background 
To ensure an accurate historical record of your project's budget, users with the required user permissions can change the status to 'Void.' When you void a budget change, you are no longer able to add adjustments to it. However, you can continue to add attachments and update the information in the 'General Information' section.
##  Things to Consider 
  * **Required User Permissions:**
    * _To void a budget change_ , 'Standard' level permissions or higher on the project's Budget tool.
  * **Additional Information:**
    * Procore recommends that your company (or each project team) develop a policy for managing budget changes in your environment.
    * Budget changes are supported by Procore's Company level [Workflows](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/workflows-company/) tool.
  * **Limitations:**
    * Budget changes do NOT automatically populate[ GC/Client invoices](https://v2.support.procore.com/glossary-of-terms), [funding invoices](https://v2.support.procore.com/glossary-of-terms), or [owner invoices](https://v2.support.procore.com/glossary-of-terms).
    * If a custom workflow has been applied to the project, you will NOT be able to manually edit the budget change status.


##  Prerequisites 
  * [Create Budget Changes](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/resource-tracking-and-project-financials-setup-guide/record-changes-on-an-internal-budget-change)


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the project's **Budget** tool.
  2. Click the **Budget Changes** tab.
  3. Locate the budget change to void in the list. Next, click its **Budget Change #** link.
  4. In the 'General Information' section, click **Edit**.  
![Budget Change Edit](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/4c192f2d386805ee9b8b5648bbf1277f/9fb60ca661bdfd5b3d6e1a9f43995558/budget-change-edit.png?fm=webp)
  5. Select 'Void' from the **Status** drop-down list.   
![Change Status To Void](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/8a27a023ac2b15e78f4f58e1cf159a21/2e39a8df3fded3dd3f0ec8dd8fef9f0b/change-status-to-void.png?fm=webp)
  6. Click **Save**.


## See Also
  * [Budget - Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/budget-project/permissions)
  * [Delete Budget Changes](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/budget-project/tutorials/delete-budget-changes)
  * [Should I update my Budget View to add RFQ values to the budget?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-should-i-update-my-budget-view-to-add-rfq-values-to-the-budget)
title: Void Budget Changes
description: How to void budget changes using the project's Budget tool.
